title: adhatoda pygmaea
intro: Adhatoda pygmaea, also known as the dwarf justicia or Adhatoda, is a small, shrub-like plant native to tropical Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in Ayurvedic medicine, where it is valued for its expectorant and bronchodilatory properties, helping to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant's leaves, roots, and flowers are used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are said to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving coughs, and improving respiratory function. In addition to its traditional uses, modern research has also explored the potential of Adhatoda pygmaea for its antimicrobial, antioxidant, and anti-inflammatory properties.
